items.title: US man arrested over Facebook posts threatening to kill Trump
items.summary: NEW YORK: An American man has been arrested for allegedly threatening to kill Donald Trump, federal officials said Wednesday, outlining a series of Facebook posts targeting the US president. "Either Trump is dead and in the ground by 2026 or I am hunting him down and putting him there," Emerald allegedly wrote in one post in May 2025, according to a Department of Justice press release. In another expletive-laden post later that month, he apparently threatened to attack Trump's resort in Florida.

items.title: Seven-month-old girl in stroller fatally shot in Brooklyn
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A seven-month-old girl in a stroller was shot and killed in Brooklyn on Wednesday by stray gunfire in a suspected gang-related shooting, top city officials said. Shots were fired in the afternoon on a Brooklyn street where police said there were several adults, two strollers and many children. The suspected shooter was in custody, Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch told reporters.

items.title: Anonymous accounts amplify divisive narratives on unregulated temples
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Social media is being used to spread divisive narratives linked to the issue of unregulated places of worship, with networks of anonymous accounts amplifying content that risks undermining social cohesion in Malaysia's multiracial society. Checks found that a ring of at least three anonymous accounts on X — with more than 6,000, 400, and 2,000 followers, respectively — consistently reposted each other's content almost daily since March 1, with some posts reaching more than 500,000 views. The accounts shared provocative material, including illustrated content and posts styled to resemble legitimate news reports, which were used to circulate misleading or unverified claims.

items.title: Weaponised misinformation fuelling online racism, experts warn
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The surge of online racism may be driven by 'weaponised misinformation and disinformation', an expert has warned. Dr Murni Wan Mohd Nor, expert researcher at the Institute of Diplomacy and Foreign Relations' (IDFR) Centre for Political Studies and Economic Diplomacy, said such content is sometimes deliberately used to create friction. "It can and must end with us," she said.

items.title: Tsunami warning issued after earthquake strikes Molucca Sea near Indonesia
items.summary: BENGALURU: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck the Northern Molucca Sea off the coast of Ternate, Indonesia, on Thursday, the United States Geological Survey said, triggering a tsunami warning for neighbouring countries. The US Tsunami Warning System said hazardous tsunami waves were possible within 1,000 km of the epicentre along coasts of Indonesia, Philippines and Malaysia. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: 7.4-magnitude quake hits eastern Indonesia, tsunami warning issued
items.summary: JAKARTA: A major 7.4-magnitude offshore quake struck in eastern Indonesia on Thursday, the US Geological Survey (USGS) said, with a US monitoring centre issuing a warning of possible "hazardous tsunami waves" within 1,000 kilometres (621 miles) of the epicentre. The quake, which was initially recorded at a magnitude of 7.8, hit at a local time of 6.48am (2248 GMT) in the Molucca Sea, USGS said. The Hawaii-based Pacific Tsunami Warning Center said that hazardous tsunami waves were possible "within 1,000 km of the epicenter" along the coasts of Indonesia, the Philippines and Malaysia.

items.title: 'I have to be strong for the children'
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Nor Nadia Abdul Majid is returning to her hometown in Batu Kurau, Perak, as she plans her life without the love and support of her husband, Amirul Hafiz Omar. Nor Nadia, 32, said her immediate concern now was to stay strong for her three children — Muhammad Aqif Amsyar, 9, Muhammad Afif Izz Rayqal, 7, and Nur Humaira, 2 — who had yet to come to terms with what happened to their father. She said her husband had been working tirelessly to provide for the family.

items.title: Seven Malaysian vessels prepare risky passage out of Hormuz
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Seven Malaysia-flagged vessels are preparing to leave the Strait of Hormuz after receiving clearance from Iran, but a veteran mariner warned the passage remains perilous. The vessels, reportedly including four oil tankers, must navigate the narrow strait that is littered with mines and other ships. Three vessels are owned by Petronas, two by MISC Bhd and one each by Sapura Energy and Shapadu Corporation Sdn Bhd.

items.title: We cannot hold: Truckers warn of rising costs, halted projects as diesel surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Malaysia’s lorry industry is taking a direct hit from surging diesel prices, with operators warning of halted construction, shrinking fleets and mounting pressure that could soon spill into higher costs across the economy. Malaysian Association of Land Lorry Drivers adviser Hairi Ali said the impact is being felt immediately on the ground, particularly among smaller operators. It will still go up,” he said, adding that operators are left with little choice.

items.title: NASA launches first crewed lunar mission in half a century
items.summary: CAPE CANAVERAL, Florida: Four astronauts blasted off from Florida on Wednesday on NASA's Artemis II mission, a high-stakes 10-day trip around the moon that marks the United States' boldest step yet toward returning humans to the lunar surface this decade before China's first crewed landing. NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket, topped with its Orion crew capsule, roared to life just before sunset at the agency's Kennedy Space Center to lift its first crew of three US astronauts and a Canadian astronaut off Earth, a thunderous ascent leaving behind a towering column of thick white vapour. The Artemis II crew of NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover and Christina Koch and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen are poised for a nearly 10-day expedition around the moon and back, taking them deeper into space than humans have ever gone.

items.title: Trump to address nation as Iran war batters approval
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump delivers a prime-time address Wednesday to Americans on the Iran war in the face of plunging approval ratings, economic jitters and spiralling diplomatic fallout. The remarks at 9pm (0100 GMT) will be Trump's first formal national speech on Iran from the White House since launching the war a month ago. Earlier Wednesday, Trump claimed a major breakthrough, saying Iran's president was seeking a ceasefire.

items.title: Trump says Iran president requests ceasefire, Teheran says 'false'
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Donald Trump said Wednesday that Iran's president had asked for a ceasefire but insisted that the Islamic republic must first reopen the Strait of Hormuz, ahead of a national address expected to touch on the state of the war. Teheran denied that President Masoud Pezeshkian had sought a truce with Washington, and announced on Wednesday evening another barrage of missile and drone attacks targeting Israel and US bases in the Gulf. Teheran has said there are no ongoing negotiations to end the war, and AFP journalists reported massive explosions in the Iranian capital Wednesday.

items.title: Ipoh hospital's cardiology team bags world title in US
items.summary: The success of the cardiology team from Ipoh's Hospital Raja Permaisuri Bainun (HRPB) at the American College of Cardiology (ACC) Conference 2026 in New Orleans has brought pride to Malaysia. The team comprising Dr Chen Tai Meng, Dr Ang Jian-Gang and Dr Yee Shen Yew outperformed other participants to emerge as world champions in the Fellows-in-Training (FITs) Jeopardy competition. "Congratulations also to Dr Nor Hanim Mohd Amin (head of HRPB'S Cardiology Department) and the entire HRPB cardiology team for their strong leadership and support.

items.title: Nadma to carry out cloud seeding to boost dam levels
items.summary: The National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) will carry out four rounds of cloud-seeding operations in Perak, Kedah, Perlis and Johor on April 6-9. "The water levels at several dams have decreased significantly, affecting water resources, the agricultural sector and daily life, particularly on vulnerable communities in rural and interior areas." Zahid said state authorities must act early to identify the needs of affected communities, including providing emergency water supplies via tanker lorries.

items.title: Thailand chokes on toxic haze
items.summary: Swathes of northern Thailand choked under a blanket of haze caused by crop burning and forest fires, putting the country's second city atop global air pollution rankings on Tuesday. Seasonal air pollution is a feature of life for much of Asia, associated with agricultural burning and weather patterns that trap pollutants. Despite the efforts, northern Thailand's tourist destination, Chiang Mai, topped the global rankings for world's most polluted major city for part of Tuesday, according to the IQAir monitor.

items.title: Japan business mood, inflation expectations rise but Mideast war clouds outlook
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan saw the business mood improve and corporate inflation expectations heighten in the three months to March, a closely watched survey showed, helping the central bank make the case for an interest rate hike as soon as this month. But firms expect conditions to worsen ahead as soaring fuel costs from the Middle East war threaten to squeeze margins, the Tankan survey showed, highlighting looming risks to a fragile economy that complicates the Bank of Japan's rate decisions. Corporate inflation expectations are also heightening.

items.title: Trump signals ceasefire talks, Teheran calls claim 'false' amid Gulf attacks
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Iran's president had asked for a ceasefire, but ruled out any truce until the vital Strait of Hormuz was reopened for crucial energy shipments. Trump made the remarks ahead of a prime-time speech at 9pm (0100 GMT Thursday) – his first since US-Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28 ignited a regional war and sparked a global energy crisis. But ahead of his national address, Trump said that Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian had asked for a truce.

items.title: Anwar calls for sustained efforts to foster a culture of knowledge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged continued efforts to nurture a culture of knowledge and revive turath texts, describing them as vital to strengthening both faith and intellectual growth. Anwar said that witnessing the launch of the work with prominent scholars underscored the ongoing legacy of the Islamic scholarly tradition across the archipelago. It showcased the intellectual treasures that have long underpinned the Islamic scholarly tradition in the archipelago," he said.

items.title: SpaceX files for US$75bil IPO, set to rocket into history
items.summary: NEW YORK: Elon Musk's SpaceX has filed confidential papers with US securities regulators for what could be the largest-ever public stock offering, a source familiar with the matter told AFP on Wednesday. The filing puts SpaceX on track to list on a public exchange by July, according to the Wall Street Journal, which cited people familiar with the matter. SpaceX did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

items.title: Amid Trump tensions, Starmer seeks closer EU cooperation for Britain's future
items.summary: LONDON: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Wednesday that Britain needed to forge a "closer partnership" with the European Union on defence, security and the economy, citing the war in the Middle East. Starmer told a news conference that "our long-term national interest requires closer partnership with our allies in Europe and with the European Union." The British prime minister has sought to rebuild post-Brexit relations with the EU since winning power in July 2024, hoping to fire up Britain's insipid economy.

items.title: Trump threatens Nato exit amid row over Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: PARIS: Tensions between the United States and Nato allies flared up as President Donald Trump said he was considering pulling the US out of the military alliance due to its European members' refusing to send ships to unblock the Strait of Hormuz. "I'll be discussing my disgust with Nato," he said of the speech. "Let me recall what Nato is," French junior army minister Alice Rufo said - though without directly addressing Trump's threat to leave Nato.

items.title: Too much AI, too little mind: The rise of 'brain fry'
items.summary: Too many lines of code to analyse, armies of AI assistants to wrangle, and lengthy prompts to draft are among the laments by hard-core AI adopters. Consultants at Boston Consulting Group (BCG) have dubbed the phenomenon "AI brain fry", a state of mental exhaustion stemming "from the excessive use or supervision of AI tools, pushed beyond our cognitive limits". "It's a brand-new kind of cognitive load," said Ben Wigler, co-founder of the start-up LoveMind AI.

items.title: Trump in audience as Supreme Court hears landmark citizenship case
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US Supreme Court was weighing Donald Trump's historic bid to end birthright citizenship on Wednesday – with the Republican president smashing protocol by sitting in the audience. Trump left the hearing following the presentation by his solicitor general, John Sauer, and did not remain for the arguments of American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) attorney Cecillia Wang, who is defending birthright citizenship. Sauer told the court that "unrestricted birthright citizenship contradicts the practice of the overwhelming majority of modern nations" and "demeans the priceless and profound gift of American citizenship."

items.title: Israeli strike on Beirut kills senior Hizbollah commander
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel killed a top Hizbollah commander on Wednesday, two sources told AFP, in a Beirut strike that Lebanon's health ministry said killed seven people. Israel's military said Hashem was Hizbollah's commander for its south Lebanon front. Another Hizbollah member, Mohammad Baqir al-Nabulsi, was also killed in the strike on the Beirut area of Jnah, the group said.

items.title: No reset in sight as Myanmar's generals reshuffle power
items.summary: WHEN Myanmar's military ousted a democratically elected government in a dawn coup on Feb 1, 2021, Ye Win Oo led the contingent of troops that arrested Nobel Peace Prize winner and the country's then-leader Aung San Suu Kyi. The key factor in Ye Win Oo's selection — the first intelligence chief to rise to the top — is his close proximity and loyalty to the outgoing commander, Min Aung Hlaing, according to six people, including diplomats, analysts and a defector. "General Ye Win Oo has gained significant power through Min Aung Hlaing's patronage, serving as a vital pillar for the leader's grip on power," said Naing Min Khant.

items.title: US Vice President Vance engages Pakistan intermediaries on Iran ceasefire
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US Vice President JD Vance communicated with intermediaries from Pakistan about the Iran conflict as recently as Tuesday, a person briefed on the matter told Reuters, a sign of his expanding role in efforts to broker an end to the conflict. At President Donald Trump's direction, Vance signaled privately that Trump was open to a ceasefire as long as certain US demands were met, the source told Reuters on Wednesday. The source said the team that Trump has said are involved in negotiations - Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, and US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner - remain involved.

items.title: NST Leader: Against all odds, SPM 2025 delivers its finest hour
items.summary: Mind you, this was the first batch of SPM candidates that did not sit for Ujian Pencapaian Sekolah Rendah in Year 6 and Pentaksiran Tingkatan 3 in Form 3. Digital classes were an experiment for the SPM students and their teachers. The SPM 2025 results not only exceeded expectations, but have also challenged the narrative of inevitable learning loss.

items.title: Trump claims Iran seeks ceasefire, insists Hormuz must reopen first
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Iran has asked the United States for a ceasefire, President Donald Trump said in a Truth Social post on Wednesday, adding Washington would consider this once the Strait of Hormuz was open. Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson said Trump's statement about a ceasefire request was false and baseless. Trump said Washington would consider the ceasefire request "when Hormuz Strait is open, free, and clear.

items.title: Soldier in ICU, six others injured after assault in camp; corporal detained
items.summary: KUANTAN: Police have detained an army corporal to assist in the investigations of an incident involving the assault of seven other military personnel, including one who sustained serious injuries. Bentong police chief Superintendent Zaiham Mohd Kahar said the 37-year-old suspect was arrested about 2.30pm yesterday following a police report lodged by a 49-year-old man, who was the father of one of the victims. He said initial investigations found that the suspect allegedly assaulted a 24-year-old victim by hitting, punching and kicking him on several parts of the body.

items.title: 14 wounded after Iranian missile fire in Israel
items.summary: BNEI BRAK, Israel: Israel's emergency services said 14 people, including an 11-year-old girl, were wounded near Tel Aviv on Wednesday during a missile attack that the military blamed on Iran. On Wednesday morning, the military said it had identified a missile launched from Yemen, as well as four rounds of Iranian missiles, which activated air raid sirens across large parts of central and northern Israel. In the central city of Bnei Brak, near Tel Aviv, the Magen David Adom emergency service said it had treated and evacuated to hospital 14 wounded people, including an 11-year-old girl in critical condition with shrapnel injuries.

items.title: Govt urged to speed up Political Financing Act
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has been urged to fast-track the gazetting and implementation of the Political Financing Act to strengthen integrity and tackle increasingly complex corruption. Malaysian Corruption Watch president Jais Abdul Karim said the move is crucial because corruption is no longer straightforward and has become harder to detect with advances in technology and the digital world. "Corruption has become increasingly complex.

items.title: Anwar: Petronas to help govt guarantee steady oil and gas supply until May
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Petroliam Nasional Bhd’s (Petronas) planning is assisting the government in providing sufficient oil and gas supplies at least until May, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. “This is very helpful to the people and the country,” he said. “The government would introduce austerity measures and a much more moderate celebration,” he said.

items.title: Anwar calls for crackdown on fake news over fuel and electricity prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The government has urged authorities, including the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), to take firm action against the growing spread of fake news on fuel and electricity prices. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that, so far, the MCMC has identified 96 pieces of false content on social media related to the issue. So far, the MCMC has identified 96 pieces of fake content on fuel and electricity prices on social media,” he said.

items.title: Long road ahead from West Asia conflict, Malaysia remains resilient, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The prolonged conflict in West Asia is expected to require a lengthy recovery period, but Malaysia remains in a relatively stable economic position to weather the challenges, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the situation has disrupted global supply chains, particularly in energy, although Malaysia has thus far managed to remain resilient. “When this happens, it will certainly affect global supply chains, especially energy, and Malaysia is not spared.

items.title: Anwar announces WFH for public sector and GLCs from April 15 to ease energy strain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The government today announced the implementation of work-from-home (WFH) arrangements for the public sector and government-linked companies (GLCs) effective April 15 as a proactive measure to address the global energy crisis. In a video message, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the decision, reached at the Cabinet meeting, aims to reduce fuel consumption and ensure the sustainability of the country’s energy supply, which has been affected by the conflict in West Asia. The prime minister also said that the government is intensifying efforts to diversify energy sources to safeguard the country’s energy supply.

items.title: Law minister confirms re-tabling of AG separation, PM term limit bill
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said following the implementation of Phase I of the Madani Accountability Framework in early 2026, the legal and institutional reform agenda is now moving into Phase II, spanning May to August. “(Also), entering Phase II beginning May, among the key initiatives to be implemented include the drafting of the Malaysian Ombudsman Bill and the Freedom of Information (FOI) Act at the next Parliamentary sitting,” she said in a post on Facebook. Azalina said among the institutional reform initiatives scheduled for implementation in April is the establishment of the Anti-Bullying Tribunal.

items.title: Who’s counting? MACC detains man after racking up RM4m in alleged fraudulent claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has detained an accounting service provider of a company for allegedly submitting documents containing false information related to travel claims worth RM4 million. Sources said the man, in his 30s, was detained yesterday while giving a statement at MACC headquarters in Putrajaya. The sources said preliminary investigations indicate the suspect allegedly committed the acts between 2023 and 2025.

items.title: Finance Ministry: Fuel subsidies maintained but BUDI95 quota reduced to 200 litres, as unsubsidised diesel breaches RM6 mark
items.summary: More critically, prices for refined products have soared, with petrol hitting US$150 per barrel and diesel reaching US$250 per barrel, placing significant upward pressure on retail prices. The other confirmed subsidised prices: Diesel (Sabah, Sarawak, and W.P. Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS): RM2.15 per litre.

items.title: Fahmi: MACC investigation into Azam Baki’s shareholdings, ‘corporate mafia’ claims to be made public
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the investigation into alleged corporate manipulation linked to MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki, including his shareholdings and claims of a “corporate mafia”, are ongoing and will be made public in due course. “As clarified by the AMLA this past Monday, the investigation is still ongoing. Fahmi also said following guidance from the Cabinet, particularly Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, any announcements will be made once the two aspects of the investigation have been completed.

items.title: Fahmi: Cabinet approves Communications Command Centre to coordinate responses to global energy crisis, all Asean meetings this year to be held online
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the Cabinet has approved the creation of a Communications Command Centre under the National Economic Action Council to coordinate government messaging on the global energy crisis and the conflict in West Asia. “The committee will be chaired by Deputy Prime Minister II Datuk Seri Ahmad Fadhilah Yusuf, and will meet regularly to coordinate government communications, particularly regarding responses and measures taken by the government and relevant agencies on the global energy crisis and the conflict in West Asia,” he said at the ministry’s weekly press conference today. Fahmi added that the Cabinet also decided that Second Finance Minister Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan and Minister of Economy Akmal Nasir will meet with the Parliamentary Special Select Committee on Finance and Economy.

items.title: SUPP president: Lily Yong’s sacking under legal process
items.summary: KUCHING, April 1 — The sacking of Sarawak United People’s Party (SUPP) Kuching chairman Datuk Lily Yong Lee Lee is currently under a “legal process”, said Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian. SUPP’s leadership has yet to address Yong’s expulsion last month along with branch secretary Voong Nam Jing. On March 26, SUPP Kuching Youth chief Nicholas Wung confirmed that the party’s Central Working Committee (CWC) had sacked the two leaders.

items.title: Fahmi says stricter penalties for fatal drunk driving not yet discussed, prosecutions ongoing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said that decisions to prosecute individuals suspected of breaking the law, including cases involving drunk drivers, fall under the authority of the Attorney General, with investigations guided by existing legal frameworks and evidence. He said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) conduct inquiries not only under road transport laws but also on broader legal grounds when sufficient evidence exists to justify prosecution. "The matter will proceed according to the Attorney General’s office regarding legal aspects,” he said during the ministry's weekly press conference here today.

items.title: MOE denies viral statement on SPM 2025 achievement by race, says deputy minister
items.summary: DENGKIL, April 1 — A viral press statement displaying the percentage of student achievement by race for the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results is untrue, said Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh. He said that the Ministry of Education (MOE) did not issue the statement, and the information was not part of the official results announcement made yesterday. Wong said the conduct of the SPM examination and the announcement of results were carried out in accordance with established benchmarks and standards, without any compromise.

items.title: PM Anwar to meet state leaders Thursday over global energy crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is set to meet all Chief Ministers and Menteris Besar tomorrow to discuss the ongoing global energy crisis and its implications for Malaysia’s economy. “God willing, there will be several press conferences in the afternoon led by Deputy Prime Minister II Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, and potentially statements from the prime minister himself following the meeting,” he said at the ministry’s weekly press conference today. The prime minister, who also serves as Finance Minister I, said the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) had held in-depth discussions on the deteriorating global energy situation and its possible impact on the country’s economy and the welfare of the rakyat.

items.title: SJKT Dengkil redevelopment ahead of schedule, completion expected early, says deputy education minister
items.summary: DENGKIL, April 1 — The redevelopment project for dilapidated buildings at Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Tamil (SJKT) Dengkil here is progressing smoothly and is ahead of schedule, with completion expected earlier than originally planned, said Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh. Wong said the project’s physical progress has reached 65.14 per cent compared to the original target of about 50 per cent in March, placing it 15.14 per cent ahead of schedule and approximately 70 days ahead of the contract timeline. The new block that houses pre-school students is almost complete as part of the Replacement Building Project (Daif 2022) at the Dengkil Tamil National Type School April 1, 2026.

items.title: CEO: Energy Commission reviews legal, regulatory frameworks to support sector stability, align with energy needs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Energy Commission (ST) is reviewing several legal and regulatory frameworks, including mechanisms for electricity cross-border trading, as part of its efforts to support energy sector stability and sustainable growth. Its chief executive officer, Siti Safinah Salleh, said the commission is currently assessing existing policies while developing new frameworks to ensure the sector remains aligned with future requirements. “We are currently reviewing and also developing several legal and regulatory frameworks to ensure that the sector remains aligned with future requirements and policies,” she said at the ST Annual Regulatory Review (STARR) 2026 here today.

items.title: Petronas operations stable, weekly fuel prices to be announced every Wednesday, says comms minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said that Petronas operations remain stable for the period between April and May, following a detailed report presented at yesterday’s National Economic Action Council (NEAC) meeting, where several aspects of the company’s operations were carefully reviewed. He also said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed that the situation continue to be closely monitored. “For the period between April and May, Petronas operations remain under control.

items.title: Johor aims to lead in FDI, strengthens Johor‑Singapore SEZ framework, says Hasni
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 1 — Johor is committed to sustaining its position as a leading investment destination and to remain at the forefront of foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows, which reached RM110.1 billion in 2025, the highest among all states. Johor State Economic and Investment Advisor Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad said the state will not remain complacent despite its strong performance, but will enhance its competitiveness and investment ecosystem continuously. He also said the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) will serve as a key blueprint to provide clarity and certainty for both new and existing investors, while also enabling the state to retain and expand current investments.

items.title: Police credit Volunteer Smartphone Patrol app for crime drop during Hari Raya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Volunteer Smartphone Patrol (VSP) application used during Op Selamat 26 has helped lower the crime index during this year’s Hari Raya Aidilfitri festive period. Bukit Aman Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department (JPJKK) director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri said the crime index fell by 111 cases, from 858 cases during Op Selamat 24 (March 29 to April 3, 2025) to 747 cases during Op Selamat 26 (March 19 to 24, 2026). Mohd Yusri said police visited 61,122 vacant houses and carried out 31,636 Mobile Patrol Vehicle (MPV) rounds during Op Selamat 26.

items.title: Socso set to enforce Gig Workers Act, urges platform providers to integrate systems for contributions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Social Security Organisation (PERKESO) is fully prepared to implement social protection for gig workers, including through system integration using Application Programming Interface (API) technology with platform providers. “This step is in line with the implementation of the Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872), which mandates all platform providers to register their gig workers with PERKESO. “This approach not only expands social security coverage but also ensures that gig workers receive appropriate protection without compromising their work flexibility,” the agency said.

items.title: Authorities say Monet painting linked to 1MDB sold, funds to return to Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Malaysian authorities have confirmed that a Claude Monet painting linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal has been sold for €25.22 million (about RM118 million). According to the New Straits Times (NST), Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Special Operations Division senior director Datuk Mohamad Zamri Zainul Abidin said the artwork and its proceeds have been identified and will be returned to the government. NST also reported that sources indicated the piece is believed to be part of Monet’s Water Lilies series, titled Nymphéas avec Reflets de Hautes Herbes .

items.title: Energy Commission to ensure supply of electricity generation feedstock despite global energy pressures, says CEO
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Energy Commission (ST) has taken steps to ensure the continued supply of feedstock, particularly gas and coal for electricity generation, as it monitors developments in West Asia with the conflict in the region entering its second month. She said cost implications would kick in as soon as June. On the Automatic Fuel Adjustment (AFA), Siti Safinah said, despite the ongoing conflicts, the AFA adjustment is projected to continue reflecting rebates up until June or July onwards.

items.title: Malaysia enforces foreign card ban on RON95 fuel at self‑service terminals nationwide, effective today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The restriction on the purchase of RON95 petrol using foreign credit and debit cards at self-service terminals at petrol stations is being enforced in stages nationwide from today. Checks in several states, particularly those bordering neighbouring countries, found that the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) is actively carrying out enforcement and monitoring operations at petrol stations. Petrol stations appear well-prepared and are complying with regulations governing the use of foreign credit and debit cards for RON95 purchases at self-service terminals.

items.title: Domestic Trade Ministry: Fuel supply stable nationwide, tighter checks in Sabah, Sarawak, Labuan
items.summary: BAU, April 1 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has ensured that fuel supply remains sufficient to meet public demand through an effective nationwide daily monitoring system, and will continue to strengthen enforcement in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan, said director-general Datuk Azman Adam. He said there have been no fuel shortages so far despite increased demand at several petrol stations following the recent festive season. He said enforcement in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan is necessary to strengthen control over fuel supply, particularly diesel, in order to ensure supply continuity, prevent disruptions, and curb any misappropriation in the market.

items.title: ‘A step towards truth’: Susanna Liew hails decision allowing access to special task force report
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Susanna Liew, the wife of missing Pastor Raymond Koh, has welcomed the government’s decision to withdraw its appeal and allow her access to the Special Task Force Report (STF Report) on his abduction. She said the move finally gives her the same treatment as Norhayati Mohd Ariffin, the wife of Amri Che Mat, who had earlier been granted access to the report. “We thank the government for treating me and Pastor Raymond Koh equally before the law.

items.title: Energy Commission: New incentive leaves 85pc of households’ electricity bills unchanged
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Energy Commission (ST) has assured that about 85 per cent of domestic households will experience little impact on their electricity bills following the implementation of the Energy Efficiency Incentive, amid the current global energy crisis. It is insulated from the energy markets, global energy markets. She reminded consumers to use energy efficiently amid the current global energy crisis.

items.title: Fisherman’s body found after falling from boat in Hutan Melintang
items.summary: IPOH, April 1 — The body of a fisherman who drowned after falling off his boat while trying to check on his propeller at Lot 125B (Pelantar 10) Hutan Melintang, near Bagan Datuk yesterday, has been found. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director (Operations) Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the body of Pee Kian Kok, 55, was found about 10 metres from the scene after the search and rescue team resumed the operations this morning using the surface searching method. The Bagan Datuk district Civil Defence Force (CDF) operations control centre said a team was mobilised to the scene for observation, but no search was conducted due to weather factors.

items.title: Customs: Absolute ban on e‑waste imports into Malaysia takes effect today
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The importation of electronic waste (e-waste) into Malaysia has officially been gazetted as an absolutely prohibited item under the First Schedule of the Customs (Prohibition on Imports) Order 2023. This follows the Federal Government Gazette of the Customs (Prohibition on Imports) Order (Amendment) (No. Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) Director-General Datuk Amran Ahmad said the move aligns with decisions made during the Special Task Force Meeting on the Direction of Management Enforcement of Plastic Waste and E-Waste Importation No.

items.title: Anwar pays tribute to JPJ on 80th anniversary, urges stronger enforcement and service quality
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid tribute to the Road Transport Department (JPJ) on its 80th anniversary, and called on the agency to further strengthen enforcement and enhance its service quality to maintain public confidence in the agency. In a Facebook post, Anwar said increasingly complex road conditions demand continued firmness, accountability and efficiency from the department. “Happy 80th anniversary to JPJ.

items.title: Penang lowers quit rent for old industrial plots after landowners protest steep hikes
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 1 — Penang has moved to ease the burden on certain landowners by reducing annual quit rent charges after complaints over steep increases under the state’s revised rates. According to Free Malaysia Today (FMT), the state executive council has agreed to lower charges for older land titles used for business and industrial purposes, particularly those without planning approval. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said such plots often involved temporary structures, depots and lorry sites, with more than 800 cases identified.
